channel.title: Comments for page "War is Child's Play"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "War is Child's Play"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll make the correction at the earliest convenience. Alternatively, consider this authorial permission to make the edit yourself if you feel so inclined. Apologies for not doing better research. Are there any other errors I need to correct?

Edit: correction made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hello, dorogoy, Victoria.

"dorogoy" is a masculine adjective. The proper feminine variant for addressing Victoria would be "dorogaya".

item.title: Re: I'll be replying to the original Author Post for updates from here on out, I think.
item.content:encoded: Content Warning / Author's Note added to the text of the page.

item.title: Re: I'll be replying to the original Author Post for updates from here on out, I think.
item.content:encoded: Would it be in poor taste to include the content warning in the author post in the story itself?

item.title: Re: Two most modest proposals.
item.content:encoded: The Interlude is now live. The mentality behind retconning the post-veil scenario is just narrative simplicity. Chapter 1 was originally posted by itself as an entry in the Broken Masquerade canon but it was lukewarmly received. This is already an 11.5k-word novella spanning about seven decades and two universes. Adding another complicating factor may not help new readers.

item.title: Re: I'll be replying to the original Author Post for updates from here on out, I think.
item.content:encoded: Interlude is live.

item.title: Re: Two most modest proposals.
item.content:encoded: I think it works fine in a not specifically defined veil-less Foundation, but if you think changing this solves some structural problem you have, then you probably ought to do so. Whatever cures the writers block, do that one.

item.title: Two most modest proposals.
item.content:encoded:
For those of you who don't hang out in IRC at ungodly hours, it may be exciting for y'all to discover that I've been trying to hammer out the next chapter. However, I'm running into an obstacle, and an idea occurred to me not too long ago on updating one of the existing chapters.

First thing I'd like to ask of anyone who has read this before or anyone lurking in Recent Posts: should I change Chapter 1 to fit into an existing post-Veil Foundationverse, should I change it to fit into baseline Foundationverse, or should I leave it as-is?

Edit: the Interlude is now live, making my second request moot.

Edit 2: would readers be interested in seeing separate rating modules for each chapter, akin to what's on the Log of Unexplained Locations?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The real crime against humanity happened outside Danner, Wisconsin on 13 October 1989 was committed by the dogs of the 22nd Armored Infantry Division during the siege of Toronto was Colonel Engel's failure to defend his city occurred as a result of sabotage of the early warning systems of both sides is my utter inability to finish this story.

I'm happy to know that you like it, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that this hasn't hit +100 yet is a crime against humanity.

+1

item.title: Re: Reader Survey
item.content:encoded: I say keep it the way it is.

item.title: Reader Survey
item.content:encoded: Who thinks it would be a good idea for me to post each chapter as its own tale and turn this page into a hub or summat?

item.title: I'll be replying to the original Author Post for updates from here on out, I think.
item.content:encoded:
This story is something of a source of shame for me because of how much it has stagnated, incomplete. I hadn't initially intended for it to be this monolithic, multi-chapter novella that it's become. And, when I decided on the route I did, I hadn't anticipated that it'd stagnate for so long. I ran headlong into writer's block that, for this particular story, persists. Maybe I was too ambitious, I don't know. I do that sometimes.

However, I knew how this story was going to end fairly early into its expansion from its initial chapter into what it is set to become. One way I work around writer's block is to chip away at different stories from the one that's got me stumped. It just so happens that I finished the epilogue to this story a long time ago. It's been sitting in my sandbox in its current form for a very long time.

Since it's been two years since I updated, I figured I might as well give you guys something to show that I haven't forgotten, that I'm still trying to work out the kinks.

Sorry this has been delayed so long. I hope y'all enjoy what's here, and I hope I can deliver the rest sometime relatively soon.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
After way too damn long, Chapter 3 is GO!

Huge thanks to TheRaven, Fae, Decibelle, minmin, and an ungodly number of other people for helping me to make this happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eugh, I've been actively avoiding bringing attention to this (it's not even listed on my author page, for example) because I've been having trouble with Chapter 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Moar plz! You've hit my feels, and I want to keep reading but I'm out of text :(

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: It just hits all my genre buttons, and I really enjoy the writing. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also worth noting that one of the ways they made use of what they already had was making dozens of variants of the AK-47 and its descendants rather than designing an entirely new gun every time. Traded some optimization for being able to fix a gun made in the 90's with parts you found in the back of the warehouse next to a stack of WWII pinup magazines, basically.

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: True. But it's not like it's Australia. Everything isn't venomous and trying to kill you. Just most things.

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: Also forgot the huge number of hazardous flora and fauna, and the crazy survivalists.

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: West Texas does consist primarily of blasted hellscape and a significant dearth of civilisation. Then once you get out of town, it gets worse.

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: Maybe he's from West Texas and was feeling homesick?

item.title: Re: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: You seem awfully happy about a story that takes place in a post-nuclear hellscape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tangent (but one of my favorite ones), but the only military skip I'd put money on against an Orange Suit would be SCP-2117. I'd love to read it, but don't quite have the balls to write it.

Belitrov is awesome, but he's still a grunt in what is probably outdated for even a White Suit. We're talking genre mismatch. He'd stand a better chance against a Fallout player character and the Brotherhood of Steel than any kind of OTT anime mecha or even Heinlein's Mobile Infantry.

item.title: Yaaaaaaaay!
item.content:encoded: Yaaaaaaaay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm, I know whatcha mean. I'll give it another reading sometime, maybe it'll click better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this thing is dialogue-heavy, I can't argue that. I'm pretty bad at "show, don't tell," and I felt letting the reader imagine what's going on while letting the folks talk about what's going on would be the best option, stylistically. I know it won't work for everyone, and if you don't like it, you don't like it, so I understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
According to Wikipedia, the DShK is 1.6 meters long. (I'm not gonna bother mentioning weight, because, for these folks, it wouldn't be an issue.) Even sawing off the barrel would still leave it big and cumbersome. A beefed-up AK, on the other hand, can be significantly smaller, and easier to move when you're moving through, say, enemy bunker complexes, which are guaranteed to be CQB situations.

Plus, the AK is probably the most reliable weapons system ever constructed, so why not use it for the end of the world? That was my reasoning, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's great to see new extensions to the Alexeiverse! +1

Still, a minor technical curiosity bugged me: why bother to re-chamber AK into 12.7mm when the Soviet already has the 12.7mm DShK machine gun? That doesn't quite suit Soviet doctrine, which always make use of what's already there first.

item.title: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded:
Minor update, all chapter names now visible.

And now, Prologue, "The Old Sailor from Massachusetts," is GO!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, REALLY want to like this, and to some extent I do… But there's something about the writing style that bugs me. The quotes are all quite long, with little to no descriptors of the speaker's manner and paralanguage; in other words, it reads like a transcription of a written conversation (a particularly well-thought-out text message exchange, maybe).

That stylistic approach would be fine, of course, except that the lack of description leads to the dialogue getting bogged down as it attempts to tell rather than show. People keep saying things that they shouldn't have to, which makes every line feel exposition-ey and breaks immersion.

The actual story is fine, though, even if I couldn't lose myself in it. I'm gonna neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. In Alexeiverse, they weren't wiped out so much as temporarily slowed down. When WWI came around, they decided that they could take advantage of the huge divisions that existed in human culture and became the entire world's R&D department. Now, they're just watching and waiting for us to get out of their way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.

Well, that does put a different spin on things. I was just going off "they made suits for the Americans, too".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why wouldn't they be profiteers? For them, it's not matter of costs and revenue, it's a matter of revenge.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. I love how much attention 2273 get; I wonder if there are any other Series III skips that have as many tales, series aside. Pairing him up with 191 was brilliant, as was expanding his backstory. I love the idea that the Engineers were war profiteers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Na. If I write him into Resurrection, it'll only be one of two things: him turning the Foundation's offer down, or a cameo, and I'm still on the fence about Resurrection anyway.

And an Orange Suit would kick his ass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for reposting this. I really enjoyed it the first time, and didn't realize it'd gone away.

Was this section added or is my memory just failing me? Site-17, January 20, 1992
Sorry in advance for the thread-jack to come, but I skimmed over that date at first, and when reading, couldn't help but think that the tale up to this point would be a good way to insert SCP-2273 into the Resurrection canon. When you read the interview as a potential recruitment for Alpha-9 instead of as a threat assessment, it still works. He should be able to hold his own against an Orange Suit…

item.title: Re: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded:
Props for doing your research - most people (well, most Americans anyway) don't know that if America hadn't nuked Japan, Russia would have rolled over the top of them in roughly 2-6 weeks. (Or that the Russians' actions trying to get there quickly are why South Korea really doesn't like communists, but that's not related to the story)

Anyway, this was fantastically written; you've done a great job of capturing the "feel" of war, both how it feels during and after, and the characterization is well-done also. You should definitely finish this.

item.title: AUTHOR POST
item.content:encoded:
Too many people to thank. Just, like, reply here to take credit.

This is meant to have five chapters; the remaining three will be added on the same way entries were added on to Incursion over an extended period. However, these two are both meant to be complementary standalones, as are the subsequent chapters.

I hope you folks enjoy reading it!

EDIT: Quick disclaimer that I should have made when I first posted this story: at points, this story deals with racial tension and outright racism; characters within this story explicitly use derogatory language and racial slurs. The opinions of these fictitious characters do not reflect my own opinions in any capacity and it is my intention that invocation of such provocative imagery positively impacts the narrative and provides insight into the characters expressing these negative opinions. I understand that it's an immensely sensitive issue and I hope it was handled in a way that is understandable
